parsed.summary: This change is shippable as-is. The v0.4.6 release introduces a BigModel error-code dispatch table, an automatic retry/backoff wrapper, and retires a chronically-broken GitHub Actions workflow in favor of a local pre-push gate. Tracing the failure paths reveals a sound implementation: (1) `classifyResultForRetry` correctly stops on success, terminal codes (`never`, `after-cooldown`, `unknown`), and retries on `immediate` or network-layer/5xx transient errors. (2) The retry loop in `withRetry` enforces a wall-clock budget that includes call duration and backoff sleep, preventing unbounded hangs. (3) HTTP 500/502/503/504 are classified as transient, while other 5xx default to `HTTP_ERROR` with `retry: unknown` (conservative stop). (4) The pre-push hook correctly loops stdin for multi-ref pushes and aborts entirely on any failing tag. Test coverage is strong (50 new tests across error classification, retry math, and integration scenarios), and all failure-path traces validate correctly without material defects.
